# Tense/Form - [form name]

<!-- One-line definition of what this form is, then a bullet list of its uses. -->
The [form name] is used to:
- [Primary use]
- [Secondary use]
- [Third use]

:::tip Polite Form Equivalent
<!-- Always include the polite-form equivalent at the start. See te-form.md and ta-form.md. -->
For polite speech, [describe the polite equivalent]:
- [plain form] → [polite form] ([meaning])
- [plain form] → [polite form] ([meaning])
:::

## Group 1 (Godan) — [Rule Summary]

<!-- State the rule briefly, then give a conjugation table. -->
[Brief explanation of how Godan verbs conjugate for this form.]

| Ending | [Form] | Pattern |
|--------|--------|---------|
| く | [form] | [k → X] |
| ぐ | [form] | [g → X] |
| す | [form] | [s → X] |
| つ | [form] | [t → X] |
| る | [form] | [r → X] |
| う | [form] | [u → X] |
| ぬ | [form] | [n → X] |
| む | [form] | [m → X] |
| ぶ | [form] | [b → X] |

:::warning Irregular Ending: [Verb]
<!-- Always call out irregular endings within the group. See te-form: 行く → いって not いいて.
     Use :::warning (not :::caution) for irregular verb exceptions — canonical pattern from te-form.md. -->
**[verb]** ([reading] - [meaning]) is irregular:
- [verb] → **[correct form]**, NOT ~~[wrong form]~~
:::

:::tip Memory Trick
<!-- Include if there's a grouping pattern to remember. -->
[Mnemonic or grouping rule for remembering the endings.]
:::

### Examples

- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])

## Group 2 (Ichidan) — [Rule Summary]

<!-- Ichidan is usually simpler — just one rule. -->
[Rule: typically "drop る, add X"]

| Ending | [Form] | Pattern |
|--------|--------|---------|
| る | [form] | ru → [X] |

### Examples

- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])
- [verb dict.] → [conjugated form] ([meaning])

## Group 3 (Irregular)

<!-- Always list する and くる explicitly. Add ある if it has an irregular form for this tense. -->

- する → [form] (to do)
- くる → [form] (to come)

### Examples

<!-- Include する compound verbs to show real-world applicability. See te-form.md.
     Common compounds: べんきょうする (to study), さんぽする (to walk), でんわする (to call). -->

- [verb する compound] → [form] ([meaning])
- [verb する compound] → [form] ([meaning])

:::caution [Verb with Exception]
<!-- e.g. ある for ない-form: ある → ない (not あらない) -->
**[verb]** is irregular for this form:
- [verb] → **[correct form]**, NOT ~~[wrong form]~~
:::
